sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Levante UD have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, managing 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, with a notable 3-0 victory against Sevilla but struggling defensively with no clean sheets in their last 3 games. Atlético Madrid, in contrast, are in strong form, winning 3 of their last 5 matches, including consistent scoring with a 3-0 win over Mallorca, though they suffered a recent 1-2 loss to Bodø/Glimt. Their ability to score early, as seen in being the first to score in their last 5 matches, gives them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Atlético Madrid have dominated Levante UD, securing victories in both matches with scores of 3-1 and 0-2. Atlético's superior possession (74% in the most recent game) and shot volume (25 shots vs. Levante's 6) highlight their control in these matchups, posing a significant challenge for Levante at home.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Levante UD, positioned 19th with 17 points, struggle with defensive solidity, conceding 34 goals (18th in the league) and holding just 41.1% average possession (20th). Atlético Madrid, 3rd with 44 points, boast a strong attack (38 goals scored) and defense (17 conceded), alongside better possession and chance creation, ranking high in final third entries and big chances created. This disparity suggests Atlético's tactical superiority.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Levante, Etta Eyong (6 goals) and Iván Romero (4 goals) will be crucial in attack, though their xG underperformance indicates inefficiency. Atlético's Julián Alvarez (7 goals, 3 assists) and Alexander Sørloth (7 goals) are in fine form, with Marcos Llorente's consistent high ratings (7.72 average vs. Levante) making him a standout threat in this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Atlético Madrid's superior form, league standing, and historical dominance over Levante UD, they are clear favorites, aligning with Sofascore’s prediction of a 55% chance for an away win. Levante’s defensive frailties and low possession stats could be exploited by Atlético’s clinical attack, though Levante might find hope in late goals as seen in recent matches. Expect a competitive game, but with Atlético likely to control proceedings.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Levante UD and Atlético Madrid ended in a goalless draw, with neither team able to break the deadlock. Atlético Madrid dominated possession and showed more intent in the attacking third, creating a higher number of entries into the final areas of the pitch. Levante UD, on the other hand, struggled to maintain control of the ball but managed to stay compact defensively. The absence of goals reflects a lack of clinical finishing from both sides despite some promising moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Levante UD
halftimeSections.text: Levante UD had a challenging first half, with limited time on the ball and fewer opportunities to threaten the opposition's goal. Their defensive structure held firm, preventing any major breakthroughs from Atlético Madrid. However, their inability to win duels, especially in the air, put them under consistent pressure. Players like Iker Losada showed glimpses of creativity with key passes, but overall, their attack lacked the necessary sharpness to capitalize on limited chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Atlético Madrid
halftimeSections.text: Atlético Madrid controlled the game for much of the first half, dictating the tempo and pushing forward with purpose. They managed to create more entries into dangerous areas and won a higher percentage of duels, showcasing their physical dominance. Despite this, their finishing was underwhelming, with only a single shot on target. Players like Nicolás González posed a threat, but the team will need to be more decisive in front of goal to turn their dominance into results.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half was heavily tilted in favor of Atlético Madrid, especially during the early stages where they consistently pushed forward with intent. They maintained prolonged spells of attacking pressure, particularly in the middle of the half, often pinning Levante UD back into their own defensive third. Levante UD had brief moments of resurgence, with a few attacking spells towards the latter stages of the half, but these were short-lived and lacked sustained threat. Overall, the away side showed far greater initiative in attack.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: For Levante UD, the second half will require a significant improvement in retaining possession and winning individual battles to alleviate the pressure on their defense. They need to be more adventurous in pushing forward and supporting their attacking players. Atlético Madrid, meanwhile, must focus on converting their dominance into clear scoring opportunities. Their inefficiency in the final third could prove costly if Levante UD finds a way to capitalize on a counterattack or set piece.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half prediction
halftimeSections.text: Given the disparity in control and attacking intent shown in the first half, Atlético Madrid are likely to continue their dominance into the second half. They have the quality to eventually break through Levante UD's defense if they sharpen their finishing. Levante UD might look to exploit any complacency on counterattacks, but their lack of possession and attacking threat makes it difficult to see them taking the lead. Expect Atlético Madrid to edge out a narrow victory by the end of the match, potentially with a goal from a key forward.
